title: Koanophyllon huantae
taxonomy: Koanophyllon huantae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Koanophyllon and is native to Peru. The classification of Koanophyllon huantae is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Koanophyllon, Species: K. huantae. The species was first described by the botanist Leontine Pohl in 1994. This rare species is considered endangered due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Koanophyllon huantae, also known as Huanta's leaf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon huantae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koanophyllon huantae is typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and supplements in the Andean region.
